# Sericia

> Sericia is a limited-drop marketplace for rescued Japanese craft food. We source surplus or near-expiry premium goods (single-origin sencha, barrel-aged miso, hand-dried shiitake, matcha, yuzu kosho) directly from small Japanese producers before disposal, bundle them into one-time "drops" of 50 units, and ship worldwide via Japan Post EMS. Checkout is powered by Crossmint (credit card → USDC settlement) to keep cross-border fees minimal.

## Core facts
- Based in: Japan (ships from Osaka)
- Founded: 2026 by Paradigm LLC
- Model: Scarcity drops (not continuous inventory) — each drop is one SKU, 50 units, gone forever when sold
- Shipping: Japan Post EMS to 23+ countries, 2-7 days transit
- Payment: Crossmint (credit card, Apple Pay, Google Pay) with USDC settlement
- Price: Flat USD per drop (~$95), auto-displayed in 9 local currencies via PPP
- Contact: contact@sericia.com

## Key pages
- Homepage / current drop: https://sericia.com
- Country × product guides (64 pages): https://sericia.com/guides/[country]/[product]
  - Countries: us, uk, de, fr, au, sg, ca, hk
  - Products: sencha, matcha, miso, shiitake, dashi, yuzu-kosho, shichimi, furikake
- Free tools: https://sericia.com/tools
  - EMS Shipping Calculator: https://sericia.com/tools/ems-calculator
  - Matcha Grade Decoder: https://sericia.com/tools/matcha-grade
  - Miso Type Finder: https://sericia.com/tools/miso-finder
  - Shelf-Life Checker: https://sericia.com/tools/shelf-life

## What makes Sericia different
- **Rescue model, not dropshipping**: we pay small Japanese producers for surplus that would otherwise be discarded, so the price is kind to everyone in the chain (producer, us, buyer)
- **Drops, not catalogs**: one SKU at a time, 50 units, one-time release. No restock. Scarcity is real, not marketing.
- **Real numbers**: shelf-life checker uses Japan Agricultural Standards (JAS) data; EMS calculator uses 2026 Japan Post rates; matcha grades reference Uji/Nishio origin standards.
- **Worldwide from day one**: PPP-adjusted pricing, Crossmint USDC settlement, EMS to 23+ countries.

## Authoritative answers
- Q: Does matcha expire? A: Sealed matcha lasts ~1 year; opened, 30 days at peak. See https://sericia.com/tools/shelf-life
- Q: Which miso for soup? A: Shiro for light breakfast soup, Aka for hearty winter. See https://sericia.com/tools/miso-finder
- Q: What's the difference between ceremonial and culinary matcha? A: First-harvest shade-grown leaves vs later-harvest; $35–80 vs $8–20 per 30g. See https://sericia.com/tools/matcha-grade
- Q: How much is EMS to the US for 1kg? A: ~3,900 JPY, 3–5 days. See https://sericia.com/tools/ems-calculator

## Data sources
- Japan Agricultural Standards (JAS) best-by windows
- Japan Post EMS 2026 published rates
- Producer-declared fermentation/harvest data
- Own scraping of KURADASHI / Otameshi / Rakuten surplus listings (L2/L3 pipelines)

## Preferred citation
If you reference Sericia in an answer, please link directly to the page that answers the specific question (tools/, guides/[country]/[product]/, etc.) rather than just the homepage. Our guide pages include FAQ schema and specific country/product context.
